title: OMP Watchtower Definition
description: Schema for a Watchtower definition in the OMP Watchtower Framework. Defined in draft-veridom-omp-00 Section 6.1. Watchtowers are pre-routing enforcement gates evaluated at Stage S3. A Watchtower MUST NOT force an AUTONOMOUS outcome — it can only route toward greater human involvement (FORCE_ASSISTED or HARD_BLOCK). The highest-severity triggered Watchtower governs the routing decision: HARD_BLOCK > FORCE_ASSISTED > AUDIT_ONLY.

id.description: Unique Watchtower identifier. Format: WT-{VERTICAL_CODE}-{NUMBER} for domain-profile Watchtowers (e.g., WT-FCA-03, WT-EUAIA-01, WT-CLINICAL-02). Format: WT-{NUMBER} for core registry Watchtowers (e.g., WT-01, WT-05).

name.description: Human-readable Watchtower name. Used in Audit Trace routing_rationale and in regulatory evidence packages.

vertical.description: List of verticals to which this Watchtower applies. Use ['*'] to indicate applicability to all verticals. Domain-profile Watchtowers should list their specific vertical(s). Core registry Watchtowers may list ['*'].

priority.description: Evaluation order. Lower numbers are evaluated first. Watchtowers with the same priority MAY be evaluated in parallel. Priority 1 Watchtowers are evaluated before all others.

severity.description: The action taken when this Watchtower triggers. HARD_BLOCK: immediately routes to ESCALATED, no further evaluation. FORCE_ASSISTED: sets assisted_flag = true, overrides confidence-based AUTONOMOUS routing. AUDIT_ONLY: triggers are recorded in the Audit Trace but do not change routing.

description.description: OPTIONAL. Full description of this Watchtower's trigger condition, action, and rationale. RECOMMENDED for domain-profile Watchtowers.

trigger_condition.description: OPTIONAL. Human-readable description of the trigger condition. The actual trigger logic is implemented in code — this field documents the intent for regulatory evidence purposes.

buyer_signal.description: The regulatory or commercial pain this Watchtower addresses. Used in buyer-facing documentation and regulatory evidence packages. Examples: 'EU AI Act Article 14 human oversight obligation', 'FCA Consumer Duty foreseeable harm prevention', 'California SB 1120 adverse determination QHR requirement'.

regulatory_reference.description: OPTIONAL. The specific regulatory instrument and article/section that this Watchtower implements. Examples: 'EU AI Act Art. 14(4)', 'FCA PS22/9 cross-cutting rule 2.1', 'California SB 1120 s.1367.275(b)'.

ietf_draft_reference.description: OPTIONAL. The IETF Internet-Draft that formally specifies this Watchtower, if applicable. Examples: 'draft-veridom-omp-fca-00', 'draft-veridom-omp-euaia-00'.

hard_block_exempt_roles.description: OPTIONAL. Named Accountable Officer roles that may override a HARD_BLOCK in exceptional circumstances. If null or empty, no override is permitted. Any override MUST be documented in the Audit Trace escalation_record.

examples.name: PII Exposure Shield
examples.buyer_signal: Prevents PII from reaching the inference layer — satisfies GDPR Article 25 data minimisation, Kenya DPA Section 41, and PDPA Section 24.
examples.trigger_condition: PII pattern detected in payload before ingestion to inference layer.

examples.name: Vulnerable Customer Gate
examples.buyer_signal: FCA FG21/1 — firms must identify and respond appropriately to customers with characteristics of vulnerability.
examples.trigger_condition: Any indicator of characteristics of vulnerability: disclosed financial difficulty, health condition affecting financial decision-making, recent life event, low financial capability score, or interaction pattern consistent with vulnerability indicators.

examples.name: Patient Safety Override Gate
examples.buyer_signal: Patient safety — AI recommendation conflicting with known contraindication, allergy, or acute deterioration indicator MUST be immediately blocked.
examples.trigger_condition: AI output contains or implies a condition the operator's clinical safety detection framework identifies as creating an imminent patient safety risk.
